summary: Six changes specced to .kbd-orchestrator/changes/ (native-kbd backend). The blocking delivery-model question was resolved by the user as self-hosted OSS, which scopes out the entire managed-SaaS operational surface (status page, on-call, DPA, SLA, incident comms) and reduces the backup story from an operated service to documentation. Three Tier-0 ship-blockers are model-independent and were specced regardless: pg_cron amd64 link fix, realtime fail-closed, one green integration run. Three Tier-1 beta-blockers follow the self-hosted bar. Two changes (c004, c006) are BLOCKED on human decisions and say so rather than guessing.
backend_rationale: OpenSpec CLI v1.4.1 is present and p14 used it correctly (5 change dirs), so the backend is real — p15's OpenSpec/native drift was process failure, not tooling. Native chosen here because all six changes are defect-driven with known root causes and fully-specified acceptance criteria; there is no proposal/design iteration for OpenSpec to add. If /kbd-apply requires an OpenSpec task surface, run `openspec new <change-id>` per change to mirror these specs.

delivery_model.consequence: Out of scope: status page, on-call rotation, DPA/ToS, SLA, incident comms. Backup becomes documentation, not an operated service. Upgrade guide becomes MORE important, not less — operators self-serve.

dependency_order: p16-c006 (needs c001; BLOCKED: does an ops runbook exist elsewhere?)

blocked_changes.blocked_on: Human must name the vulnerability-report recipient. A SECURITY.md pointing at an unmonitored inbox is worse than none.
Human must confirm whether a backup/restore runbook exists outside this repository. If yes, the change collapses to a doc link plus verification; if no, it is days of genuine engineering.

open_questions_carried: Re-tag v1.0.0 or ship v1.0.1? Still unanswered across assess, analyze, and spec. Blocks any release action.
Does the beta include realtime subscriptions? c002 assumes yes (README advertises them; ListenChangeSource makes them deliverable). If no, the honest move is removal, not repair.
Is v0.10.0 → v1.0.0 actually a supported upgrade? p35-c004 landed a BREAKING PgBackend::acquire change.
next_action: /kbd-plan p16-v1.0-release-closure — order the six changes and assign the wait budget. Note that c001/c002 are independent and could run in parallel; c003 is the wait-budget risk (0/8 green today, failures beyond the build step unknowable).
